APPEARANCES:
Gilliam F. Stewart of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for intervenors / moving parties Intervenors St. Paul and Fire Marine Insurance Company, Wausau Insurance Company, Argonaut Insurance Companies, National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA, Certain Solvent London Market Insurance Companies, on behalf of their suspended insured, Defendant Scott Co. of California
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Susan Jacobson as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Mary Fish, deceased

RULING:
INTERVENORS ST. PAUL AND FIRE MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY, WAUSAU INSURANCE COMPANY, ARGONAUT INSURANCE COMPANIES, NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA, CERTAIN SOLVENT LONDON MARKET INSURANCE COMPANIES, ON BEHALF OF THEIR SUSPENDED INSURED, DEFENDANT SCOTT CO. OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors St. Paul and Fire Marine Insurance Company, et al., on behalf of Scott Co. of California's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue One (negligence) and off calendar as to Issue Two (products liability). As to Issue One, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that it did not owe decedent a duty of care. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal, use, handling and/or disturbance of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of an employee. Pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates in favor of the creation of a duty in the present matter. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden. As to Issue Two, plaintiffs have dismissed their products liability causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
